records.ety: [OE. rumpe; akin to D. romp trunk, body, LG. rump, G. rumpf, Dan. rumpe rump, Icel. rumpr, Sw. rumpa rump, tail.]

defs: The end of the backbone of an animal, with the parts adjacent; the buttock or buttocks.
Among butchers, the piece of beef between the sirloin and the aitchbone piece. See Illust. of Beef.
The hind or tail end; a fag-end; a remnant.
quotes: The Rump abolished the House of Lords, the army abolished the Rump, and by this army of saints Cromwell governed.
